results.title: Commodity Strategy ETF (COMD) - Global X ETFs
excerpts:
Section Title: COMD > Reasons to Consider COMD > Active Management, Deep Expertise
Content:
COMD’s portfolio managers leverage deep expertise in commodity market analysis and futures trading in pursuit of excess returns, while adapting to evolving market conditions—without the burden of K-1s.
 ... 
Section Title: COMD > ETF Summary
Content:
The Global X Commodity Strategy ETF (COMD) is an actively managed ETF that seeks to provide exposure to commodity assets. The Fund gains indirect exposure to commodities through commodity futures contracts and exchange-traded products that invest in physical commodities (“Physical Commodity ETPs”). For portfolio construction, the portfolio managers typically utilize quantitative macro, trend, and momentum models (the “Models”) developed by Wealthspot LLC, an affiliate of the Adviser, to assist in security screening and analysis, though the managers exercise ultimate investment discretion.
 ...

Section Title: ... > ### About ICICI Prudential Commodities Fund Direct Growth
Content:
ICICI Prudential Commodities Fund Direct Growth is a Equity Mutual Fund Scheme launched by ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund. This scheme was made available to investors on 12 Oct 1993. Lalit Kumar is the Current Fund Manager of ICICI Prudential Commodities Fund Direct Growth fund. The fund currently has an Asset Under Management(AUM) of ₹11,24,266 Cr and the Latest NAV as of 07 Apr 2026 is ₹47.48.
The ICICI Prudential Commodities Fund Direct Growth is rated Very High risk. Minimum SIP Investment is set to ₹100. Minimum Lumpsum Investment is ₹5,000. Exit load of 1% if redeemed within 3 months.
;
Section Title: ICICI Prudential Commodities Fund Direct Growth > ... > Investment Objective
Content:
The scheme seeks to generate long-term capital appreciation by creating a portfolio that is invested predominantly in Equity and Equity related securities of companies engaged in commodity and commodity related sectors.
Fund benchmark NIFTY Commodities Total Return Index
[Scheme Information Document(SID)](https://www.icicipruamc.com)

Section Title: Commodity
Content:
Historically, commodities like oil, wheat, and metals have performed well during inflationary periods, offering income and growth potential even in bearish markets. Current trends show increased interest in energy commodities due to the global transition, while precious metals and agricultural products remain popular as safe-haven assets during geopolitical instability. According to the Bloomberg Commodity Index, commodities have shown notable returns in recent inflationary cycles, making them an attractive option for portfolio balance. While commodities can provide valuable diversification, they come with unique risks, including price volatility, geopolitical exposure, and regulatory shifts that can impact supply and demand. Investors should focus on screening metrics like historical performance, expense ratio, and volatility measures to find high-quality commodity funds.
 ... 
Section Title: Commodity
Content:
Investors should focus on screening metrics like historical performance, expense ratio, and volatility measures to find high-quality commodity funds. Funds with low correlation to equities, strong returns in past inflationary periods, and exposure to stable commodity sectors (e.g., precious metals, energy) offer valuable diversification and inflation-hedging potential. Last Updated: 09/11/2025 View more View less

Section Title: 5 Leading Commodities Mutual Funds in India
Content:
Here are five [leading commodity mutual funds](https://www.equitymaster.com/the-fundstrategist/detail.asp?date=12/19/2025&story=11&title=3-Leading-Metal-Mutual-Funds-for-2026&utm_source=the-fundstrategist&utm_medium=website&utm_campaign=e-letter&utm_content=the-fundstrategist) that investors could keep on their watchlist.
Section Title: 5 Leading Commodities Mutual Funds in India > \ SBI Gold Fund
Content:
[SBI Gold Fund](https://www.personalfn.com/mutual-funds/best-funds/factsheet/sbi-gold-g-direct-plan?utm_source=the-fundstrategist&utm_medium=website&utm_campaign=e-letter&utm_content=the-fundstrategist) offers targeted exposure to Gold. It seeks to provide returns that closely track those of the SBI Gold Exchange-Traded Scheme.
As of 31 December 2025, the fund's asset allocation is 98.73% in commodities, with 1.27% in cash and cash equivalents.
The fund assets under management stand at Rs 107.75 billion (bn) as on 31 December 2025.
A systematic investment plan (SIP)-based approach is effective here because it averages costs while managing volatility.
 ... 
Section Title: 5 Leading Commodities Mutual Funds in India > \ SBI Gold Fund > \ Tata Resources & Energy Fund
Content:
Fund managers select stocks after rigorous fundamental research into factors such as management, business competitiveness, corporate governance, growth prospects, and past performance.
The fund currently has about 98.46% of its portfolio invested in equities, with the remaining 1.54% is held as cash and cash equivalents.
The portfolio adopts a balanced market-cap mix, with 54.17% in large caps for stability, while midcap (18.7%) and small-cap (27.13%) exposure adds higher growth from commodity upcycles.
As of 31 December 2025, the fund held 23.19% of AUM in metals and mining, positioning it to benefit from the sharp January surge.
Oil, gas, and consumable fuels accounted for 19% of the portfolio, providing additional exposure to energy-linked price cycles.
 ... 
Section Title: 5 Leading Commodities Mutual Funds in India > \ SBI Gold Fund > \ ICICI Pru Commodities Fund
Content:
[ICICI Prudential Commodities Fund](https://www.personalfn.com/mutual-funds/best-funds/factsheet/icici-pru-commodities-fund-g-direct-plan?utm_source=the-fundstrategist&utm_medium=website&utm_campaign=e-letter&utm_content=the-fundstrategist) aims to generate long-term returns by investing in equity and equity-related securities of companies engaged in commodity and commodity-related sectors.
The fund is currently about 96.16% invested in equities and debt (0.15%), with the remaining held as cash and cash equivalents (3.7%).
Its portfolio is more balanced, with 36.32% invested in large caps, 34.17% in midcaps, and the remainder in smallcaps (29.51%).
Section Title: 5 Leading Commodities Mutual Funds in India > \ SBI Gold Fund > \ ICICI Pru Commodities Fund
Content:
Within the sector allocation, metals and mining account for the largest weight at 40.62%, positioning the portfolio to benefit meaningfully from commodity price cycles. This is followed by chemicals (28.25%), construction materials (15.56%), capital goods (8.33%), and even foreign equity (3.67%).
The fund allocation to Vedanta, a diversified play on the commodity upcycle, stood at 8.72%. Jindal Steel (8.37%), followed by JSW Steel (7.46%), Jindal Stainless (7.19%), and Ambuja Cement (6.03%).
The allocation suggests a clear tilt toward cyclical beneficiaries, with the portfolio positioned to capture broad-based gains across metals, materials, and industrial demand.
 ... 
Section Title: 5 Leading Commodities Mutual Funds in India > \ SBI Gold Fund > \ Kotak Nifty Commodities Index Fund
Content:
[Kotak Nifty Commodities Index Fund](https://www.personalfn.com/mutual-funds/best-funds/factsheet/kotak-nifty-commodities-index-fund-reg-g?utm_source=the-fundstrategist&utm_medium=website&utm_campaign=e-letter&utm_content=the-fundstrategist) tracks the Nifty Commodities Index.
The Nifty Commodities Index is a sectoral index that tracks the performance of companies that derive a significant portion of their revenue from commodity-related businesses.
It includes stocks from segments such as metals, oil and gas, chemicals, cement, and fertilisers, offering a consolidated view of how commodity-linked sectors perform in the Indian equity market.
As of 31 December, power accounted for 20.08% of the asset allocation, followed by petroleum products (18.73%), cement and cement products (15.21%), ferrous metals (14.28%), and non-ferrous metals (6.15%).
Section Title: 5 Leading Commodities Mutual Funds in India > \ SBI Gold Fund > \ Kotak Nifty Commodities Index Fund
Content:
This offers investors diversified exposure across energy, metals, and core industrial sectors, reducing reliance on a single commodity cycle while staying aligned with broader economic demand trends.
The fund is tilted towards largecap which accounted for 80.80% of the portfolio, followed by mid-cap stocks (19.20%).
This positioning is mirrored in stock selection. Reliance Industries accounted for 9.88% of the portfolio, offering exposure across the oil-to-chemicals value chain. This is followed by NTPC at 7.49%, reflecting the theme of rising energy demand.
Tata Steel stood at 7.14%, while UltraTech Cement (6.17%) and Hindalco (6.15%) providing exposure to materials and construction-linked demand.
